reasoning: The FRED query shows the DEXKOUS value as of June 6, 2025, is 1360.28, which is the most recent baseline before June 8. The exchange rate has recently experienced a strong short-term downward trend, dropping from ~1381 in late May to ~1360 in early June, driven by shifting global macro forces and interventions. Bank analysts from late 2024 forecasted the rate in the mid-1400s, suggesting fundamental long-term support for a weaker won, but the near-term momentum and daily linear drift (-1.56 per day) suggest further downside testing before any stabilization. Under a standard random walk currency model, confidence in any specific direction decreases with time, so probabilities should revert toward 50%. Given the active negative short-term momentum, I estimate the likelihood of the rate being higher on June 15 is <50% (around 42%), with the probability gradually regressing toward 50% for the increasingly distant horizons (July, September, and December).
